notes: Root cause of the three paywall-ambush issues: Reflect's first-visit hard-paywall trigger (app/(tabs)/reflect.tsx) ran in a mount-only useEffect that called router.push('/paywall') synchronously before the Reflect screen itself had painted a frame, and — since expo-router keeps tab screens mounted when inactive — the same effect could still fire later even after the user had already switched to another tab, because nothing cancelled it on blur. That produced exactly the reported symptoms: tapping Reflect showed a stale/duplicate Home frame or the paywall instead of Reflect content, and tapping Settings shortly after could get the paywall sprung on top of it instead. Fixed by switching to useFocusEffect with a 900ms setTimeout that is cleared on blur/unmount, so (a) Reflect's own content renders and is visible for a beat before the hard paywall covers it, and (b) navigating away before the timer fires cancels it outright instead of letting it fire on whatever tab is now active. Also fixed: Home's fully-locked anchor cards (Midday/Evening for free users) previously showed every habit row with a lock icon + 'Pro' badge, giving zero preview of what the anchor contains; those rows now render with plain unlocked-looking text/icon (still routing to the paywall on tap) while the distinct 'over the free habit limit within your one accessible anchor' case keeps the heavier lock/Pro treatment, since that's a different lock reason. Also centered the first onboarding slide's content vertically (added flex: 1 to its container) to remove the large dead space below the Start button. npx tsc --noEmit is clean.
